title: Structural Changes Additional Information - Markland POA
description: Please provide comprehensive details about your structural modification project. All structural changes require professional design and engineering oversight, plus Markland ARB approval prior to construction.

primary.summary: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval. All new construction must conform to the Markland Declaration of Covenants and Restrictions as well as all applicable governmental codes, ordinances, regulations, permits and laws. The ARB evaluates each proposed improvement application for total effect, including the manner in which the lot is developed.
keyRequirements: Complete Design Review Application Form must be submitted
Refundable Performance Deposit required (amount varies by project scope)
Site Plan, Landscape Plan, and Architectural Plans required
No site work can commence until site plan approved and builder meets with Developer representative onsite
ARB approval required before any construction begins
primary.quote: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval. No site work can commence until a site plan has been approved and the builder has met with a representative of the Developer onsite.

additionalReferences.summary: Comprehensive construction hour restrictions, site access requirements, and construction activity standards
keyProvisions: Construction hours comply with St. Johns County regulations
Clean construction site maintenance required at all times
Debris and trash removal with regular dumpster emptying
All construction activities must be controlled within property boundaries

additionalReferences.summary: Building height, setback, and architectural consistency requirements for all structures
keyProvisions: Maximum building height 35 feet from lowest point of finish grade
No home shall exceed three stories including attic rooms
Minimum setbacks per St. Johns County engineering plans and Southaven PUD
Consistent architectural style required - no mixing of various styles within one home

fields.label: Primary Type of Structural Change
options: New Window/Door Opening in Exterior Wall
fields.description: Select the primary type of structural modification being performed

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.1 Maximum Building Height and Stories
relevantBylaws.requirement: Maximum building height 35 feet, no home shall exceed three stories including attic rooms
relevantBylaws.note: All structural additions must comply with Markland height restrictions and architectural consistency requirements

fields.label: Markland Lot Type Classification
options: Traditional Type 1 Lot (58 ft width with rear garage access)
Traditional Type 2 Lot (73 ft width with rear garage)
fields.description: Lot type determines specific design requirements and restrictions

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 3 Lot Types and Section 6.2.5 Minimum/Maximum Home Square Footage
relevantBylaws.requirement: Different lot types have specific minimum/maximum square footage requirements and design standards
relevantBylaws.note: Lot classification affects setbacks, architectural requirements, and allowable square footage

fields.label: Additional Work Included
fields.description: Check any additional work being performed as part of this project

fields.label: Purpose/Use of New/Modified Space
fields.placeholder: Family room, master bedroom suite, home office, guest quarters, etc.
fields.description: Describe the intended use of the new or modified space

fields.label: Current Home Square Footage
fields.placeholder: Enter current square footage
fields.description: Total existing living space square footage

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.5 Minimum and Maximum Home Square Footage
relevantBylaws.requirement: Each lot type has specific minimum and maximum square footage requirements
relevantBylaws.note: Addition cannot exceed maximum square footage limits for your lot type

fields.label: Square Footage Being Added
fields.placeholder: Enter additional square footage
fields.description: Total new conditioned living space being added

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.5 Minimum and Maximum Home Square Footage
relevantBylaws.requirement: Total home size after addition must comply with lot-specific maximum square footage limits
relevantBylaws.note: 73 ft lots: max 3,500 sf, 63 ft lots: max 3,200 sf, 58 ft lots: max 2,800 sf

fields.label: Height and Elevation Details
fields.placeholder: Ceiling heights, floor elevations, roof peak heights, etc.
fields.description: Provide specific height measurements and elevation details

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.1 Maximum Building Height and Section 6.2.2 Finish Floor Elevation
relevantBylaws.requirement: Maximum height 35 feet measured from lowest point of finish grade. 2-step requirement for floor elevations
relevantBylaws.note: All finished floors must have one step from grade to porch, one additional step into home

fields.label: Foundation Type
fields.description: Type of foundation for the new construction

relevantBylaws.requirement: Minimum exposed foundation shall be 18 inches, varies by lot type. Positive drainage required.
relevantBylaws.note: Foundation work must adhere to Neighborhood Grading and Drainage Plan

fields.label: Framing Materials
fields.description: Primary framing materials and construction method

fields.label: Roofing System
fields.placeholder: Roof structure, materials, pitch, drainage, etc.
fields.description: Describe roofing structure, materials, and integration with existing roof

relevantBylaws.requirement: Approved materials: Asphalt shingles (30-year minimum), concrete/clay tile, slate, standing seam metal. Minimum 6/12 slope.
relevantBylaws.note: All roof penetrations must be painted to match roof color. Roof style must be consistent with home.

fields.label: Exterior Finish Materials
fields.placeholder: Siding, brick, stucco, stone, trim materials, etc.
fields.description: Describe all exterior finish materials and how they match existing home

relevantBylaws.requirement: Approved materials: stucco, stone, tabby natural shell, brick, wood siding, Hardi-Plank. No vinyl or aluminum siding.
relevantBylaws.note: Dominant front elevation materials must wrap around all four sides. Two color minimum scheme required.
relevantBylaws.quote: All exterior finishes and all exterior color alterations must be reviewed and approved by ARB prior to installation.

fields.label: Major Structural Elements
fields.placeholder: Beams, columns, load-bearing walls, footings, etc.
fields.description: Detail key structural components and load-bearing elements

fields.label: Total Lot Size
fields.placeholder: e.g., 0.25 acres or 10,890 sq ft
fields.description: Total lot size in acres or square feet

fields.label: Front Setback Distance
fields.placeholder: Distance in feet from front property line
fields.description: Distance from addition to front property line

relevantBylaws.requirement: Minimum front setback generally 20 feet from front lot line for conventional homes. ARB may require larger setbacks.
relevantBylaws.note: Must comply with St. Johns County engineering plans and Southaven PUD Zoning Ordinance

fields.label: Side Setback Distances
fields.placeholder: Left: __ feet, Right: __ feet
fields.description: Distance from addition to both side property lines

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.4 Setbacks and Section 7.6 Site Walls & Terraces
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must comply with minimum setbacks per approved engineering plans. No site wall within 3 feet of property line.
relevantBylaws.note: Corner lots have additional side facade design requirements when facing a street

fields.label: Rear Setback Distance
fields.placeholder: Distance in feet from rear property line
fields.description: Distance from addition to rear property line

fields.label: Current Lot Coverage Percentage
fields.placeholder: Enter current percentage
fields.description: Current percentage of lot covered by structures

fields.label: Proposed Lot Coverage Percentage
fields.placeholder: Enter proposed percentage
fields.description: Total lot coverage percentage after addition

fields.label: Height Restriction Compliance
fields.description: Does the addition comply with local height restrictions?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 6.2.1 Maximum Building Height and Stories
relevantBylaws.requirement: Maximum 35 feet from lowest point of finish grade, no more than three stories including attic rooms
relevantBylaws.note: Height restrictions are strictly enforced - ARB will not approve structures exceeding limits

fields.label: Is an architect required for this project?
fields.description: Many structural additions require architectural design

relevantBylaws.requirement: Accurate, scaled architectural drawings required with floor plans, all exterior elevations, materials, and design details
relevantBylaws.note: ARB evaluates applications for total effect and aesthetic impact

fields.label: Architect Name and Firm
fields.placeholder: John Smith, AIA - Smith Architecture LLC
fields.description: Name and firm of licensed architect (if required)

fields.label: Architect License Number
fields.placeholder: FL-AA12345
fields.description: State architecture license number

fields.label: Structural Engineer Required
fields.description: Most structural additions require structural engineering

fields.label: Structural Engineer Name
fields.placeholder: Jane Doe, PE - Structural Solutions Inc.
fields.description: Name and firm of licensed structural engineer

fields.label: Structural Engineer License Number
fields.placeholder: FL-PE67890
fields.description: State professional engineer license number

fields.label: General Contractor
fields.placeholder: ABC Construction Company
fields.description: Name of licensed general contractor performing the work

fields.label: General Contractor License
fields.description: State general contractor license number

fields.label: Contractor Insurance Coverage
fields.placeholder: General liability: $X, Workers comp: $Y, Carrier: XYZ Insurance
fields.description: Contractor insurance details including liability and workers compensation

fields.label: ARB Application Status
fields.description: Current status of Markland ARB application

relevantBylaws.requirement: Required information must be submitted in its entirety to the ARB prior to any review, construction and/or purchase of materials
relevantBylaws.note: ARB approval is mandatory before any construction begins

fields.label: Performance Deposit Status
fields.description: Refundable performance deposit required for all construction

relevantBylaws.requirement: Refundable Performance Deposit required - checks made payable to Markland POA. Deposit amounts posted on Design Review Application Form.
relevantBylaws.note: Performance deposit refunded upon successful completion and compliance verification

fields.label: Pre-Construction Site Meeting
fields.description: Required onsite meeting with Developer representative

relevantBylaws.requirement: No site work can commence until site plan approved and builder has met with a representative of the Developer onsite
relevantBylaws.note: Mandatory coordination meeting required before any construction activities begin

fields.label: Building Permit Status
fields.description: Current status of required building permits

fields.label: Building Permit Number(s)
fields.placeholder: Enter permit numbers if obtained
fields.description: Provide permit numbers if permits have been issued

fields.label: Other Permits Required
fields.description: Check any additional permits required for this project

fields.label: Zoning Variance or Special Approval Required
options: Variance required but not yet applied
fields.description: Does this project require any zoning variances or special approvals?

fields.label: Planned Construction Start Date
fields.description: When do you plan to begin construction?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Construction hours shall comply with St. Johns County regulations. Developer and Association reserve right to limit weekend and holiday hours.
relevantBylaws.note: No construction may begin until ARB final approval and pre-construction meeting completed

fields.label: Estimated Construction Duration
fields.description: Estimated time to complete construction

fields.label: Target Completion Date
fields.description: When do you expect construction to be completed?

fields.label: Construction Work Hours
fields.placeholder: Monday-Friday 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Saturday 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM, No Sunday work
fields.description: Specify days and hours when construction work will occur

relevantBylaws.requirement: Construction hours must comply with St. Johns County regulations and codes
relevantBylaws.note: Developer and Association may limit weekend and holiday construction hours as residents occupy community

fields.label: Construction Phases
fields.placeholder: Phase 1: Foundation (weeks 1-3), Phase 2: Framing (weeks 4-7), etc.
fields.description: Outline major construction phases and timeline

fields.label: Electrical Service Upgrade Required
fields.description: Will the addition require electrical service upgrades?

fields.label: Plumbing Connections Required
fields.description: Check all plumbing connections required for the addition

fields.label: HVAC System Modifications
fields.placeholder: Ductwork extensions, system capacity upgrades, zoning, etc.
fields.description: Describe any heating and cooling system modifications needed

fields.label: Existing Utility Line Impacts
fields.placeholder: Any existing utility lines that need to be relocated or protected
fields.description: Address impacts to electric, gas, water, sewer, cable, phone lines

relevantBylaws.requirement: Owner/Builder must coordinate utility relocations in advance as part of initial site plan submittal and bear all associated costs
relevantBylaws.note: Must call for utility locates to avoid damage to existing utilities. Repairs paid by Owner/Builder causing damage.

fields.label: Tree Removal Required
fields.description: Will any trees need to be removed for this addition?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.5 Site Preparation - Existing Vegetation and Tree Protection
relevantBylaws.requirement: Protected trees must be identified on plans and properly protected with silt barriers. No disturbance within drip line of protected trees.
relevantBylaws.note: Tree protection fencing required per St. Johns County regulation. Conservation land disturbances strictly prohibited.

fields.label: Drainage System Modifications
fields.placeholder: Changes to roof drainage, yard grading, storm water management, etc.
fields.description: Describe all drainage and grading changes needed

relevantBylaws.requirement: Must adhere to Neighborhood Grading and Drainage Plan. Positive drainage required - cannot be diverted onto adjacent lots.
relevantBylaws.note: Downspouts must include subsurface drain pipe or splash blocks. Finished floor elevations cannot be modified without approvals.

fields.label: Soil and Geotechnical Considerations
fields.placeholder: Soil test results, foundation requirements, etc.
fields.description: Describe any soil testing or geotechnical engineering requirements

fields.label: Neighbor Impact Assessment
fields.placeholder: Visual impact, privacy concerns, construction disruption, etc.
fields.description: Assess potential impacts on neighboring properties

fields.label: Builder's Risk Insurance
fields.description: Builder's risk insurance protects against construction-related damages

fields.label: Construction Safety Plan
fields.placeholder: Safety fencing, signage, pedestrian protection, equipment staging, etc.
fields.description: Describe safety measures for workers, residents, and neighbors

relevantBylaws.requirement: Maintain clean and orderly construction site. All activities controlled within property boundaries. Material storage and equipment staging on-site only.
relevantBylaws.note: Continuous silt fencing and protective barriers required. Portable restrooms must face lot, not street.

fields.label: Emergency Access Considerations
fields.placeholder: Maintain emergency vehicle access, clear fire lanes, etc.
fields.description: How will emergency access be maintained during construction?

relevantBylaws.requirement: Traffic flow must never be blocked or restricted on neighborhood streets as result of parking and construction activities
relevantBylaws.note: Emergency access must be maintained at all times during construction

required_documents: Complete architectural plans and elevations (ARB required)
Site survey showing existing conditions and proposed addition
Building permit application or approved permits
Tree survey and protection plan (if applicable)
Completed Markland Design Review Application Form
Color and material samples for ARB review

criticalReminders: NO CONSTRUCTION may begin until ARB final approval is received - this is mandatory and strictly enforced
Pre-construction meeting with Developer representative required before any site work begins
Performance deposit must be posted before construction can commence
All structural changes must comply with maximum building height of 35 feet and 3-story limit
Lot-specific minimum and maximum square footage limits must be observed for additions
All exterior materials and colors require ARB approval before installation
Construction hours must comply with St. Johns County regulations and community standards
Continuous silt fencing required along conservation lands, wetlands, lakes, and common areas
All construction activities must remain within property boundaries
commonViolations: Starting construction without ARB approval - results in immediate stop-work order
Exceeding maximum building height or story limitations
Adding square footage that exceeds lot-type maximums
Using non-approved exterior materials or colors
Failing to maintain clean construction site and proper debris removal
Construction activities extending onto adjacent properties or common areas
Not coordinating utility relocations in advance of site plan submittal
Inadequate tree protection and conservation land encroachment
Not maintaining required setbacks from property lines
Missing required professional team members (architect/structural engineer for major additions)

arbProcessNotes.applicationTimeline: Allow 30-45 days for ARB review after complete application submission
requiredMeetings: Pre-construction site meeting with Developer representative (mandatory)
Potential ARB presentation meeting if requested
Final inspection meeting for performance deposit release
arbProcessNotes.performanceDepositInfo: Deposit amounts vary by project scope and are posted on the Design Review Application Form. Deposit is refundable upon successful completion and compliance verification.
arbProcessNotes.arbContactInfo: Contact ARB for latest application requirements and forms - requirements subject to change
